commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a compact live status dashboard to the tray flyout, extends tray snapshots with freshness and preview data, adds a Diagnostics tray action, and adds focused dashboard-summary tests plus a proof screenshot.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a new tray UX feature PR rather than a current-main bug. Source inspection confirms current main lacks the compact tray glance, and the contributor's screenshot shows the branch behavior.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] This is a draft feature PR with no narrow automation repair; maintainers need to decide the UX/product direction and wait for completed CI.

target.title: Add tray live dashboard glance
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the tray flyout exposed actions and rich sub-flyouts, but the top-level surface did not provide a compact live status summary.
- Why it matters: operators should be able to see gateway health, freshness, session activity, node/session counts, and usage at a glance before choosing a deeper action.
- What changed: added a render-free `TrayDashboardSummaryBuilder`, a dashboard glance card in the tray menu, snapshot fields for heartbeat/preview data, a Diagnostics action, and unit coverage for summary behavior.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no gateway protocol changes, no capability permission changes, no tray right-click behavior changes, and no MCP server behavior changes.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs / instructions
- [x] Tests / validation
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore / infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Tray / WinUI UX
- [ ] Windows node capability
- [ ] Local MCP / `winnode`
- [ ] Gateway / connection / pairing
- [ ] Setup / onboarding
- [ ] Permissions / privacy / security
- [x] Tests / CI / docs

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] Related to a bug or regression

## Validation

- `./build.ps1` — passed; built Shared, Cli, WinNodeCli, SetupEngine, WinUI.
- `dotnet test ./tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests.csproj --no-restore` — passed: 2513 passed, 31 skipped, 2544 total.
- `dotnet test ./tests/OpenClaw.Tray.Tests/OpenClaw.Tray.Tests.csproj --no-restore --filter "FullyQualifiedName~TrayDashboardSummaryBuilderTests"` — passed: 48 passed, 0 skipped, 48 total.
- `dotnet test ./tests/OpenClaw.Tray.Tests/OpenClaw.Tray.Tests.csproj --no-restore` — passed: 1263 passed, 0 skipped, 1263 total.

## Real behavior proof

- Environment tested: Windows ARM64 worktree `bkudiess-tray-live-dashboard`, direct unpackaged WinUI app from this branch.
- Exact steps or command run:
  - `./run-app-local.ps1 -NoBuild -AllowNonMain`
  - Opened the OpenClaw tray flyout from the notification area.
  - Captured and cropped the connected-state tray dashboard.
- Evidence after fix:

  ![Connected OpenClaw tray dashboard](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/bkudiess/openclaw-windows-node/bkudiess-tray-live-dashboard/.github/pr-proof/tray-live-dashboard-connected.png)

  - Screenshot shows the new top-level dashboard glance: `Connected · localhost:18789`, freshness (`Updated 17s ago`), node/session summary (`1/1 node · 1 session`), main session/model, existing Gateway/Windows Node/Sessions/Usage rows, and the new `Diagnostics` action.
  - Safe diagnostic log line from isolated proof run: `{"event":"app.start","metadata":{"nodeMode":false,"useSshTunnel":false}}`.
  - Focused dashboard summary tests validate the health/heartbeat/metrics/session-preview summary behavior.
- Observed result: the tray flyout displays a readable live dashboard summary above the existing detailed rows and action list.
- Not verified / blocked: N/A
## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

No bot review conversations exist yet; this is a draft PR.
